title: hieracium maculatum subsp. candelanum
intro: Hieracium maculatum subsp. candelanum, also known as spotted hawk's beard or spotted hawkweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion tribe within the sunflower family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties due to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and other compounds. The health benefits of Hieracium maculatum subsp. candelanum include reducing inflammation, treating wounds, and potentially having antimicrobial and antiviral effects. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat various health issues, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
